comment: Steam Controller Bridge config. With the SDL 3 backend, standard buttons/sticks/triggers/d-pad and the trackpad (as right stick) map themselves -- no index tuning needed. Copy this to config.json to customize.

device.comment: Pick which controller to use if several are present. name_contains is matched case-insensitively against the controller name; first match wins. Leave as 'steam' for the Steam Controller.

output.comment: Emulated pad on startup: 'xbox' or 'ds4'. Switchable at runtime from the tray.

remap.comment: Button remapping. Each entry is OUTPUT button (left) <- PHYSICAL source (right). Default is identity (A<-A etc). Set a source to "NONE" to disable an output. Two outputs may share one source. Run `run.bat --probe` to see which physical buttons your controller has and what they're called. PHYSICAL sources: A B X Y LB RB BACK START GUIDE LS RS DUP DDOWN DLEFT DRIGHT, plus Steam Controller extras MISC1 MISC2 LPADDLE1 RPADDLE1 LPADDLE2 RPADDLE2 (grip/back buttons). Example: "A": "RPADDLE1" makes the right grip button act as A.

gyro.comment: Gyro aiming (off by default). destination: 'right_stick' or 'mouse'. activation: 'always', 'hold' (only while activation_button held -- a 'gyro ratchet'), or 'off'. activation_button is a PHYSICAL button name (so you can use a grip paddle): A B X Y LB RB BACK START GUIDE LS RS DUP DDOWN DLEFT DRIGHT MISC1 MISC2 LPADDLE1 RPADDLE1 LPADDLE2 RPADDLE2.

tuning.comment: stick_deadzone (0..1) stops drift; poll_hz is the update rate.
